topic.title_cn: 总结你当前的价值与优先级
topic.prompt_en: Summarize your current values and priorities.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to describe present values and priorities in a concrete, non-abstract way.

angles.title: State the time frame
content: Tell the listener what period of life you are reflecting on.
That makes change and continuity easier to follow.

angles.title: Describe the change
content: Say what has actually changed in habits, mindset, or priorities.
Use contrast to make the shift visible.

angles.title: Explain what shaped it
content: Mention the experience, decision, or pressure that influenced the change.
That gives the reflection a real cause.

angles.title: End with your current direction
content: Finish with who you are now or where you think you are heading.
Leave room for uncertainty when that sounds more honest.

sequence.target: State the period you are reflecting on and the biggest shift.
Explain what drove the change and how it unfolded.
End with your present priorities, identity, or future direction.

high_score_tips: Reflection sounds stronger when you compare then and now clearly.
Name one cause of change instead of using vague personal growth language.
A modest ending often sounds more mature than a perfectly confident future plan.
If the future is uncertain, say that directly and calmly.

content.problem: Talking only in general growth language
content.why_it_hurts: The answer feels vague if there is no visible contrast between the past and now.
content.fix: Use one habit, decision, or value that changed clearly over time.

content.why_it_hurts: Reflection is weaker when the listener cannot see what shaped it.
content.fix: Mention the pressure, event, or realisation behind the shift.

content.why_it_hurts: That can sound less honest than a realistic, open-ended answer.
content.fix: Allow room for change if that matches your real situation.

language.wrong_example: I become more better at handling pressure.
language.better_example: I have become better at handling pressure.
language.reason: Use the present perfect for growth that continues until now, and do not double the comparative.

language.wrong_example: In future I will focus more on balance.
language.better_example: In the future, I will focus more on balance.
language.reason: The article makes the phrase more natural in standard spoken English.

core.example: I would naturally use "change" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "growth" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "priority" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "direction" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "reflection" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "identity" when talking about this topic.

extended.note: Useful when reflecting on change over time.
Good for present identity and future answers.
Useful for growth and long-term reflection.

core.example: I would use "over the past few years" naturally in this answer.
I would use "I have become more" naturally in this answer.
I would use "these days" naturally in this answer.
I would use "I am still figuring out" naturally in this answer.

core.phrase: looking back, I can see that
core.example: I would use "looking back, I can see that" naturally in this answer.

core.phrase: what matters to me now is
core.example: I would use "what matters to me now is" naturally in this answer.

extended.example: I would use "the biggest shift has been" when making the answer sound more natural.

extended.phrase: I no longer feel the need to
extended.example: I would use "I no longer feel the need to" when making the answer sound more natural.

extended.phrase: at this point in my life
extended.example: I would use "at this point in my life" when making the answer sound more natural.

extended.phrase: I want to leave room for change
extended.example: I would use "I want to leave room for change" when making the answer sound more natural.

framework.goal: Reflect on change through contrast, cause, and current direction.

moves: State the time frame and the main contrast.
Show the listener what has changed.
Explain the cause or turning point behind the change.
Use one example that shows the shift in a concrete way.
End with your current priorities or future direction.
If needed, leave room for uncertainty.

delivery_markers: At this point in my life, ...

samples.answer:
The values and priorities that shape the way i make decisions now is something I can talk about quite naturally. If I had to summarise my current values and priorities, I would say I care most about clarity, steadiness, and meaningful use of time. That does not mean I always live perfectly by those values, but they are the standard I come back to when things feel scattered. For example, I now respect consistency more than intensity, and I value peace of mind more than looking busy from the outside. Those priorities affect small choices as well as bigger ones, from how I plan my week to what kind of work I accept.

What made it worth talking about was not only the event or idea itself, but also the effect it had on the way I think or act. So my values are not just ideas I agree with. They shape the rhythm of my life in a practical way. Overall, it is a topic that feels real to me because it connects to ordinary life rather than a perfect story.
highlights: Band 6 answer matched to the 总结你当前的价值与优先级 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

opening: The topic that comes to mind is the values and priorities that shape the way I make decisions now.
I would first explain the background clearly.
body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.
After that, I would show the result or practical effect.
closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: It mattered because So my values are not just ideas I agree with. They shape the rhythm of my life in a practical way.
upgrades.why: A specific reason sounds much more natural than a general claim.
upgrades.note: Use the personal effect to close the answer.

upgrades.improved: For example, I now respect consistency more than intensity, and I value peace of mind more than looking busy from the outside.
upgrades.why: A clear turning point gives the answer shape and movement.
upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

samples.answer:
The topic that comes to mind for me is the values and priorities that shape the way I make decisions now. If I had to summarise my current values and priorities, I would say I care most about clarity, steadiness, and meaningful use of time. That does not mean I always live perfectly by those values, but they are the standard I come back to when things feel scattered. What stood out most was that for example, I now respect consistency more than intensity, and I value peace of mind more than looking busy from the outside.

After that, those priorities affect small choices as well as bigger ones, from how I plan my week to what kind of work I accept. What I find interesting about it now is that the situation seemed fairly ordinary at the time, but it revealed something more lasting about how I handle pressure, choices, or relationships. So my values are not just ideas I agree with. They shape the rhythm of my life in a practical way. So if I were explaining this in a speaking test, I would focus less on sounding dramatic and more on why the experience or idea genuinely stayed with me.

samples.answer:
If I had to answer this topic in a more reflective way, I would still begin with the values and priorities that shape the way I make decisions now. If I had to summarise my current values and priorities, I would say I care most about clarity, steadiness, and meaningful use of time. That does not mean I always live perfectly by those values, but they are the standard I come back to when things feel scattered. The turning point, for me, was that for example, I now respect consistency more than intensity, and I value peace of mind more than looking busy from the outside.

From there, those priorities affect small choices as well as bigger ones, from how I plan my week to what kind of work I accept. What matters now is not the surface event or statement alone, but the shift in perspective behind it. So my values are not just ideas I agree with. They shape the rhythm of my life in a practical way. In that sense, the topic feels useful because it shows not only what happened or what I think, but also how my judgment has become a little more precise over time.

practice.task: Summarize your current values and priorities.
checklist: Compare the past and present clearly.
End with your current position or a realistic future direction.
self_prompts: How certain are you about the future?
